mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers the historical Buddha's life, the Four Noble Truths, the Eightfold Path, dependent origination, nirvana, and the spread of Buddhism across Asia — mapping to the founding of Buddhism (A.2), cultural context (A.1), and traditional beliefs and doctrines (C.1).

Zen Buddhism covers its origins in Chinese Chan, Bodhidharma, koans, sudden enlightenment, Rinzai and Soto schools, and influence on Japanese culture — directly relevant to Buddhism in Japan (A.5) and Mahayana schools (C.2.b.iii).
Daoism's relationship with Buddhism in China, including their synthesis and mutual influence, is relevant to Buddhism in Central Asia and China (A.4).
The Silk Road's role in spreading Buddhism from India to Central Asia and China through Kushan intermediaries directly addresses Buddhism in Central Asia and China (A.4).
Shinto's historical interaction with Buddhism in Japan directly addresses the relationship between indigenous Japanese religion and Buddhism (A.5).
The samurai's engagement with Zen Buddhism including the tea ceremony directly addresses Buddhism in Japan (A.5) and the religious and cultural role of Buddhist art (C.4).

Schopenhauer's incorporation of Buddhist concepts including suffering and the will into Western philosophy addresses Buddhist philosophy's role and contribution (B.3) through cross-cultural reception.
Buddhist nirvana is directly discussed alongside other afterlife conceptions — relevant to the goal of the Eightfold Path: Nirvana (C.1.c).
